DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE OCT-29-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF AE RETAIL WEST, LLCS, SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO ALLIED UNIVERSAL; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO QUASH DENIED. REQUESTS 14 TO 16 ARE REASONABLY TAILORED TO THE ISSUES IN THIS LITIGATION AND MAY BE PRODUCED SUBJECT TO THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. FOR SF CENTRE TO ASSERT TRADE SECRETS FOR MATERIALS IT SHARED WITH ALLIED UNIVERSAL, IT MUST SHOW THAT THERE WERE CONTRACTS THAT APPLIED TO RESTRICT THIS INFORMATION, OR TRADE SECRET PROTECTION WOULD HAVE BEEN WAIVED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS). THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: CAROL HARABURDA, CSR# 8052 (TEL: 925-360-6065 / EMAIL: CHREPORTER@COMCAST.NET). (525/JPT)

Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Renewal Fees Paid

Motion To Quash Plaintiff Ae Retail West, Llcs, Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Allied Universal
Off Calendar Oct-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MATTHEW HOUGHTON, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (tel: 415-543-8700) for Plaintiff AE Retail West, LLC.
Atty. ANAND SINGH, Esq. of Sanders Roberts LLP (tel: 213-426-5000) for Defendant S.F. Centre Limited Partnership.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's Motion To Quash Plaintiff Ae Retail West, Llcs, Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Allied Universal.

Defendant's counsel is contesting the tentative ruling. The parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem and authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf. The Court notes counsel is not personally present in the courtroom as required. The Court exercises its discretion and allows counsel to present oral argument. Matter argued and submitted. The Court notes it is not making any finding as to the concept of region. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to quash DENIED. Requests 14 to 16 are reasonably tailored to the issues in this litigation and may be produced subject to the protective order. For SF Centre to assert trade secrets for materials it shared with Allied Universal, it must show that there were contracts that applied to restrict this information, or trade secret protection would have been waived.

The Court will prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR# 8052 (tel: 925-360-6065 / email: chreporter@comcast.net). (525/JPT)

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And For Leave To Sue Receiver
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Continued To Nov-21-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Carly Alameda (Receiver), Farella Braun & Martel LLP, 415-954-4400 via zoom
Matthew Houghton Esq., Reed Smith LLP, 415-54-8700, via zoom for plaintiff
Anand Singh Esq., Sanders Roberts LLP, 213-426-5000, via zoom for defendant

Mr. Singh seeks clarification of the hearing date of both motions. The Court confirms that both motions will be heard on NOV-21-2024. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF AE RETAIL WEST, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND FOR LEAVE TO SUE RECEIVER

is continued to November 21, 2024. No later than October 25, 2024 AE Retail West, LLC to file a motion in action CGC-23-609424 for leave to sue the receiver and set if for November 21, 2024 hearing. Any opposition to that motion shall be filed and served no later than November 8, 2024. Any reply shall be filed no later than November 15, 2024. The Court will hear both motions on November 21, 2024.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported. Raquel Sharp CSR 10619, 925-226-8222, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com (D501)
